cases.passage: Rahul and Aryan were partners in a firm carrying on the business of manufacturing and selling garments. Their partnership deed was silent on the matter of interest on goodwill. On 1st April 2018, Aryan retired from the firm and received Rs. 1,00,000 as goodwill. The profits of the firm for the year ended 31st March 2019 were Rs. 1,50,000. The remaining profits were divided between Rahul and the remaining partners in the ratio 3:2. On 1st April 2019, Rahul brought in his friend, Karan, as a new partner with 1/4th share in the profits. The goodwill account was adjusted against the capital of Karan. The profit for the year ended 31st March 2020 was Rs. 2,00,000. What amount would be credited to the capital account of Karan?

questions.q: What was the amount received by Aryan as goodwill on retirement?
questions.explanation: As per the passage, Aryan received Rs. 1,00,000 as goodwill on retirement.

questions.q: What was the profit sharing ratio between Rahul and the remaining partners?
questions.explanation: As per the passage, the profit sharing ratio between Rahul and the remaining partners was 3:2.

questions.q: What was the share of Karan in the profits of the firm for the year ended 31st March 2020?
questions.explanation: As per the passage, Karan had 1/4th share in the profits, so his share in the profits for the year ended 31st March 2020 would be (1/4) * Rs. 2,00,000 = Rs. 50,000.

questions.q: What was the amount credited to the capital account of Karan?
questions.explanation: As per the passage, the goodwill account was adjusted against the capital of Karan, which is Rs. 50,000.

questions.q: What was the profit of the firm for the year ended 31st March 2019?
questions.explanation: As per the passage, the profit of the firm for the year ended 31st March 2019 was Rs. 1,50,000.

cases.passage: Sachin and Rakesh were partners in a firm carrying on the business of manufacturing and selling electrical goods. Their partnership deed was silent on the matter of interest on goodwill. On 1st April 2018, Sachin retired from the firm and received Rs. 90,000 as goodwill. The profits of the firm for the year ended 31st March 2019 were Rs. 1,80,000. The remaining profits were divided between Rakesh and the remaining partners in the ratio 2:3. On 1st April 2019, Rakesh brought in his friend, Rohan, as a new partner with 1/3rd share in the profits. The goodwill account was adjusted against the capital of Rohan. The profit for the year ended 31st March 2020 was Rs. 2,50,000. What amount would be credited to the capital account of Rohan?

questions.q: What was the amount received by Sachin as goodwill on retirement?
questions.explanation: As per the passage, Sachin received Rs. 90,000 as goodwill on retirement.

questions.q: What was the profit sharing ratio between Rakesh and the remaining partners?
questions.explanation: As per the passage, the profit sharing ratio between Rakesh and the remaining partners was 2:3.

questions.q: What was the share of Rohan in the profits of the firm for the year ended 31st March 2020?
questions.explanation: As per the passage, Rohan had 1/3rd share in the profits, so his share in the profits for the year ended 31st March 2020 would be (1/3) * Rs. 2,50,000 = Rs. 83,333.

questions.q: What was the amount credited to the capital account of Rohan?
questions.explanation: As per the passage, the goodwill account was adjusted against the capital of Rohan, which is Rs. 83,333.

questions.q: What was the profit of the firm for the year ended 31st March 2019?
questions.explanation: As per the passage, the profit of the firm for the year ended 31st March 2019 was Rs. 1,80,000.
